tldr: These tools solve different problems — Fyne is a Go GUI toolkit, Rectangle is a macOS window manager — so this is a workflow shift, not a code port.
why_switch: You want keyboard and drag-to-snap window management on macOS, not to build GUI apps
Rectangle is free, open source, and zero-config to get started
Native-feeling drag zones and shortcuts on macOS
Pro version offers cascade and custom grids
what_to_export: List of Fyne apps you maintain (kept separate)
Existing macOS window-management shortcuts you rely on
Any prior window manager config (Spectacle, Magnet, Yabai)
Backup of current macOS keyboard shortcut settings
export_method: There is nothing to extract from Fyne for use in Rectangle. Instead, document the macOS window-management workflow you want to keep: shortcuts, snap zones, multi-monitor behavior. If you previously used Spectacle, you can keep its shortcut map as a reference — Rectangle was modeled on Spectacle and supports importing its shortcut layout per the official Rectangle docs.
import_method: Install Rectangle from the official site or Homebrew. On first launch, choose either the recommended or Spectacle-compatible shortcut preset. Adjust drag-to-snap zones in Preferences and grant the Accessibility permission required for window control. Your Fyne apps continue to build and run unchanged; Rectangle just manages their windows.

concept_mapping.note: Rectangle moves and resizes any standard macOS window
Free version covers halves and quarters
Use Yabai/Hammerspoon if you need scripting

gotchas: Rectangle is not a GUI toolkit; Fyne app code does not migrate
Accessibility permission must be granted or shortcuts silently fail
Hotkey collisions with apps and macOS Mission Control are common
Multi-monitor behavior may need manual preference tweaks
Free version lacks custom grids — verify Pro features in the official docs before relying on them

email.subject: Switching my Mac to Rectangle for window management
email.body:
Hey,

I'm setting up Rectangle as the window manager on my Mac so I get keyboard snapping and drag zones without buying Magnet. This is unrelated to our Fyne apps — those keep shipping from the same Go codebase.

If you want the same setup, install via Homebrew, grant Accessibility permission, and pick the default shortcut preset.

Thanks
